what_it_does: This file is a generated surface specification for view F3, describing a palette / node-library surface organized by node kind, and linking it to journeys, platforms, UI addresses, and build targets.
observations: Generated surface-spec for view F3 in the J1 journey spine directory.
Title is 'Palette / node library (by kind)' and it represents NODE-kinds, NODE-types, and INTRO-cap.
Platforms are desktop and mobile; status is planned.
Home journey is J1 and journeys list contains only J1; cross_journeys is empty.
Lists four concrete UI addresses under ui://canvas/node, ui://chat/node-palette, ui://rail/palette, and ui://rail/palette/node-kinds.
build_to names components canvas, chat, and rail; a design_lint command; and a tokens source design/_system/tokens.json -> design-system.css.
The metadata states it was built by a Claude Code session and warns never to invent an address.
standouts: Strict instruction that addresses must not be invented and must come from the listed set.
build_to includes both a lint gate and a single token source described as 'the only look-source'.
cross_journeys is empty, so this surface is currently isolated within J1.
concerns: Status is 'planned', so the surface is not yet implemented.
cross_journeys is empty, which may mean no cross-journey web connections are defined yet.
The design_lint command references a fail-on condition that is not detailed in this file.
notes: A planned, J1-local surface spec that maps a node-kind palette to four UI addresses and ties it to canvas/chat/rail build targets plus a token-driven style source.
questions: What is the exact semantic difference between the four listed addresses?
How is the design_lint 'fail-on' criteria defined and enforced?
Which Claude Code session generated this and where is the generation log?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it documents a palette/node-library surface by kind and connects it to journeys, addresses, and build outputs.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to create a canonical, reusable specification for a node palette so that node kinds/types have stable UI addresses and build targets across canvas, chat, and rail surfaces.
novelty: The explicit 'never invent an address' rule and the inclusion of a lint gate plus a single token source as build targets are distinctive for a surface spec.
contribution: It contributes a structured, address-bound definition of the F3 palette surface that links conceptual node categories to concrete UI locations and build-time checks, which a fused system could keep as a navigable design substrate.
summary_for_embedding: A planned surface spec for view F3, a desktop/mobile palette of node kinds/types under journey J1, listing four UI addresses and build targets for canvas, chat, and rail components.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a canonical, address-linked blueprint for a node-kind palette surface that guides implementation and linting across canvas, chat, and rail.
